title: WIP - DO NOT MERGE - Make pull-always always pull
body:
I've changed the vendor piece of c/storage that will need to change
with this commit.  If @mtrmac is agreeable with this concept, I'll
work up a proper PR for c/storage.

The motivation for this changes is we currently say in our docs that
`--pull-always` always pulls from the registry regardless of what's
locally stored.

That turns out to NOT be the case, we do reuse some of the images/layers
if they are present locally.

This change passes the `--pull-always` flag down to the c/storage code
so it can decide what to do.

comments.body:
I've some vendor shenanigans going on.  However, if folks could review, especially @mtrmac and @redhatdan, in particular for the change in c/storage/copy.go, I'd appreciate it.

If the approach is OK, I'll work up a change in c/storage and then will vendor it into here before merging.

What’s the end-user-relevant outcome of the difference? Sure, the difference is end-user-observable (is the pull fast/slow), but why does the user care?

@mtrmac the main reason for me doing the change is our doc says "always pulls" for `--pull-always` and the code is not doing that.  I'm just fore seeing a user report that we're not pulling the image, but using the local bits if present.  Perhaps I'm over thinking.  

Secondarily, this was a weekend "fun" project for me to see what I could do with it.

My mind I'm happy to close this, but if we do, I think our docs/man pages needs a little touch up to explain a bit more how we're doing the pull-always.  Maybe later, if we get a user ping, we can add a "--pull-refresh" or some such that would ignore all the local layers/images and just pull from the repo.

Thoughts?

> My mind I'm happy to close this, but if we do, I think our docs/man pages needs a little touch up to explain a bit more how we're doing the pull-always.

Sure, something to the effect “contact the registry to ensure exactly the same image is present locally” might be useful.

>  Maybe later, if we get a user ping, we can add a "--pull-refresh" or some such that would ignore all the local layers/images and just pull from the repo.

I’m not at all sure that would work:
- We can’t just delete local layers and pull them again with the same IDs, because some of the layers might be shared with other images that we must not break.
- If we pulled the same local layers with different IDs, that’s probably possible in principle (unless some code assumes that layer ID == DiffID, not sure), but some parts of Podman, like history/tree(/prune?) assume parent’s layers are a prefix of child’s layers, and it would be very unintuitive for a `--pull-refresh` to change output/behavior of such commands.
I am going to close this as I don't believe it is relevant any longer..
